summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the service on Radical Faith Offering Sunday, framing the day as a call to step into radical faith and action. He announced the church's plan to purchase and renovate a second location in South Scottsdale, sharing inspiring stories of early, generous commitments from church families. The core of the message was an exploration of the biblical 'Law of the Harvest,' outlining five key spiritual principles. Pastor Travis taught that everything starts as a seed, a seed must be planted to produce, and while we sow the seeds, God supernaturally grows the harvest. He emphasized that we reap exactly what we sow and that the quantity of our sowing directly impacts the size of our harvest. Throughout the sermon, he challenged the congregation to move beyond passive prayer into active, faith-filled planting, especially in areas of personal need. He connected this principle directly to the day's offering, urging everyone to ask God for a radical, faith-stretching amount to give toward the new campus, trusting that God cannot be out-given. The message concluded with a call to worship and an invitation for those needing miracles in their lives—whether for healing, family restoration, or freedom—to begin planting seeds of faith through their obedience and generosity.
mainPoints: The Law of the Harvest governs spiritual life: you must plant a seed to ever expect a harvest.
You reap exactly what you sow; the character and quantity of your planting determines your harvest.
Radical faith requires action—trusting God by planting seeds, especially in areas of personal need, and believing He will provide a supernatural return.
The Law of the Harvest governs spiritual life: you must plant a seed to ever expect a harvest.
You reap exactly what you sow; the character and quantity of your planting determines your harvest.
Radical faith requires action—trusting God by planting seeds, especially in areas of personal need, and believing He will provide a supernatural return.

versesMentioned.text: Very truly I tell you, unless a kernel of wheat falls to the ground and dies, it remains only a single seed. But if it dies, it produces many seeds.

He also said, "This is what the kingdom of God is like. A man scatters seed on the ground. Night and day, whether he sleeps or gets up, the seed sprouts and grows, though he does not know how. All by itself the soil produces grain—first the stalk, then the head, then the full kernel in the head."
In the same way, faith by itself, if it is not accompanied by action, is dead.
One person gives freely, yet gains even more; another withholds unduly, but comes to poverty. A generous person will prosper; whoever refreshes others will be refreshed.

Do not be deceived: God cannot be mocked. A man reaps what he sows. Whoever sows to please their flesh, from the flesh will reap destruction; whoever sows to please the Spirit, from the Spirit will reap eternal life.
Give, and it will be given to you. A good measure, pressed down, shaken together and running over, will be poured into your lap. For with the measure you use, it will be measured to you."

Remember this: Whoever sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and whoever sows generously will also reap generously. Each of you should give what you have decided in your heart to give, not reluctantly or under compulsion, for God loves a cheerful giver. And God is able to bless you abundantly, so that in all things at all times, having all that you need, you will abound in every good work.

"Truly I tell you," Jesus replied, "no one who has left home or brothers or sisters or mother or father or children or fields for me and the gospel will fail to receive a hundred times as much in this present age: homes, brothers, sisters, mothers, children and fields—along with persecutions—and in the age to come eternal life."

Monday.scripture: John 12:24 - "...unless a kernel of wheat falls to the ground and dies, it remains only a single seed. But if it dies, it produces many seeds."
Monday.action: Identify one 'seed' (time, encouragement, a financial gift) you've been holding onto and prayerfully commit to planting it this week.
Monday.prayer: Lord, give me the courage to let go of what I'm clutching so it can become something fruitful for Your kingdom.

Tuesday.scripture: James 2:17 - "...faith by itself, if it is not accompanied by action, is dead."
Tuesday.action: Take one tangible step today that demonstrates your faith in God's provision, whether it's an act of generosity or a step of obedience you've been postponing.
Tuesday.prayer: God, transform my good intentions into active faith. Help me to trust You enough to move.

Wednesday.scripture: Proverbs 11:24 - "One person gives freely, yet gains even more; another withholds unduly, but comes to poverty."
Wednesday.focus: The Principle of Sowing and Reaping
Wednesday.action: Intentionally sow a 'seed' of kindness, appreciation, or help into someone's life today, with no expectation of immediate return.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, rewire my heart towards generosity. Let me experience the joy of giving freely as You do.

Thursday.scripture: Galatians 6:7 - "Do not be deceived: God cannot be mocked. A man reaps what he sows."
Thursday.action: Reflect on an area where you are experiencing a negative 'harvest' (stress, conflict). Identify and commit to changing the 'seeds' you are sowing in that area.
Thursday.prayer: Holy Spirit, reveal the connections between my actions and my outcomes. Give me wisdom to sow seeds that lead to life and peace.

Friday.scripture: 2 Corinthians 9:6 - "Remember this: Whoever sows sparingly will also reap sparingly, and whoever sows generously will also reap generously."
Friday.focus: The More You Sow, The More You Grow
Friday.action: Review your giving (time, talent, treasure). Ask God if there's an area where He is calling you to increase your 'sowing' to a new level of faith.
Friday.prayer: Lord, stretch my faith. Challenge me to sow more generously, trusting in Your promise of a generous harvest.

Saturday.scripture: Luke 6:38 - "Give, and it will be given to you. A good measure, pressed down, shaken together and running over..."
Saturday.action: Prepare for worship tomorrow by deciding in advance how you will respond to God's prompting to give, serve, or encourage. Act on it as an act of worship.
Saturday.prayer: God, I choose to trust You with my seeds. I believe You are a multiplier. Prepare my heart to worship You with radical obedience.
